delta.description: Intake skill improves assertion pass rate from 24% to 100% (+76 percentage points)

analyst_observations.detail: All 7 assertion types discriminate well between with-skill and without-skill. No assertion passes equally in both configurations.

Without-skill baseline passes some assertions in hotfix (3/7) and refactor (2/7) scenarios — Claude natively recognizes urgency and suggests tests-first. These are 'capability uplift' areas where the base model partially delivers. The skill's unique value is in STRUCTURE and CLASSIFICATION, not raw capability.

The 5 assertions that baseline passes are all about domain knowledge (rollback for hotfix, tests for refactoring). The 16 that only with-skill passes are about PROCESS STRUCTURE: intake brief format, work type classification, phase selection, workflow selection, approval gates. This confirms the 'encoded preference' hypothesis from the GPT review.

The skill's strongest differential is in 'produces-intake-brief' (0/3 baseline vs 3/3 with-skill) and 'asks-before-proceeding' (0/3 vs 3/3). Without the skill, Claude never produces a structured triage document and never asks for approval before starting work.
